text: The surgeon performs dissection of adhesions and enterolysis, carefully separating the adhered tissues within the surgical field.
The surgeon performs primary closure of the abdominal wall defect following the resection of the hernia sac, preparing for the placement of a dual mesh.
The surgeon dissects multiple adhesions to expose the hernia sac, preparing the surgical field for further repair.
The surgeon identifies a three-centimeter defect at the upper edge of the mesh and closes it using non-absorbable stitches, securing the mesh to the fascial margin.
A larger intraperitoneal dual mesh prosthesis is placed to cover the entire abdominal wall, following the primary closure of the wall defect.
